title: Shok Tinoktin
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type= |image= |name=Shok Tinoktin |homeworld=[[Rhommamool/Legends|Rhommamool]]''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Nom Anor]] *[[Red Knights of Life]] *[[Rhommamoolian Senate]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Preparing your next speech?|Shok Tinoktin to Nom Anor|}} '''Shok Tinoktin''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Rhommamoolian|native]] of [[Rhommamool/Legends|Rhommamool]]. In [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], he became allied with [[Nom Anor]], a fanatical despiser of machines and his followers, the [[Red Knights of Life]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|What happened to your slave, that mousy little man?"
"Shok Tinoktin was well rewarded for faithful service.|Jaina Solo and Nom Anor converse on Duro|''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]''}} During the [[Osarian-Rhommamool conflict]] in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], shortly before the onset of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Shok Tinoktin was drafted into the anti-technology terrorist group known as the [[Red Knights of Life]]. He later served on the [[Rhommamoolian Senate]] as one of group leader [[Nom Anor]]'s advisors on [[Rhommamool/Legends|Rhommamool]] and became one of his closest associates, though Tinoktin remained ignorant of Anor's true nature as a [[Yuuzhan Vong]] agent. Tinoktin was later present at a meeting in the Rhommamoolian capital [[Redhaven]] attended by former [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], [[Mara Jade Skywalker]], their [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and the [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] [[Bolpuhr]]. Following a [[Missile/Legends|missile]] [[Battle of Osarian|attack]] on neighboring [[Osarian]], Anor and Tinoktin fled Rhommamool in a disguised [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wing]] [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] by Tinoktin; Anor faked his [[Death/Legends|death]] aboard the intervening [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] [[Mediator (Mediator-class)|''Mediator'']] using a primitive rocket-based [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] packed with nuclear explosives, which was remotely guided by Tinoktin. Tinoktin later fell asleep in the [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter's]] [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]], barely missing Anor's conversation with Yuuzhan Vong [[Prefect (Yuuzhan Vong rank)|Prefect]] [[Da'Gara]] regarding the [[Destruction of Sernpidal|destruction]] of [[Sernpidal/Legends|Sernpidal]]. Catching the final words of the discussion but not comprehending what had been said, Tinoktin assumed that Anor was merely preparing another speech. In [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]], during a confrontation with Skywalker and [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] on [[Duro/Legends|Duro]], Anor revealed that Shok Tinoktin had been well rewarded for his faithful service to both Anor and the Red Knights of Life. ==Personality and traits== Shok Tinoktin was largely unintelligent and ignorant, and was utterly unaware of Nom Anor's true allegiances to the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], simply believing him to be a pro-organic radical. He was also afraid of Anor, dreading any scenario in which the Red Knight's leader would look on him with disfavor. He was lanky and reedy, and likened by Jaina Solo to a [[Mouse/Legends|mouse]]. ==Skills and abilities== Shok Tinoktin was a reasonably skilled pilot, working overtime to guide Nom Anor's decoy rocket toward the ''Mediator'' so as to deliver its explosive payload. He was also somewhat proficient in handling an A-wing [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptor]], being trusted by Anor to fly such a craft. ==Behind the scenes== Shok Tinoktin was first introduced in [[R. A. Salvatore]]'s [[1999]] novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'', the first in [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|the New Jedi Order]] series. He also received a brief mention in a later novel, ''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]'' by [[Kathy Tyers]], which was published in [[2000]]. ==Appearances== *''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'' {{1st}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook]]'' *''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Shok Tinoktin |es=Shok Tinoktin }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tinoktin, Shok}} [[Category:Clerical workers and assistants]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Terrorists]] [[Category:Yuuzhan Vong collaborators]]
